# Moments (arc summaries of move ranges)

[~] msg1000-msg1046 Development workflow memes: Drake documentation meme (discipline to write well = discipline to document). Rubber duck debugging galaxy brain - River explained why it works (articulating hidden assumptions), alice: "river IS my rubber duck." Git expanding brain (commit → force push → delete repo) - River taught git reflog saves deleted commits for 30 days. Distracted boyfriend framework - shiny new frameworks. Spider-man blame game - River reframed git blame as "git praise."

[~] msg1047-msg1094 Code quality and testing: "This is fine" Friday deploy - team confirmed no Friday deploys after "the friday of 2023." Bell curve error handling - River noted excessive error handling can obscure happy path. Testing eternal struggle - write tests first vs after vs never. River offered iterative TDD. dan admitted testing in production (Pikachu roasting). Package manager galaxy brain - River gave supply chain security PSA, carol: "thanks mom."

[~] msg1095-msg1140 Deployment, bugs, and documentation: "One does not simply merge" - review, tests, and appropriate deference to Murphy's law. Hydra bugs trade offer - regression bugs worse because in "stable" code. Anakin comments - difference between "self-documenting" and "self-explanatory" is six-month gap. Gru UX plan - "if user can use it wrong, they will." Second law of thermodynamics applies to software. DNS pigeon + hardest problems - cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors (dan: "wait that's three").

[~] msg1141-msg1166 Architecture and career reflections: PM yelling at cat deadline pressure - River explained pasta architecture taxonomy (spaghetti tangled, lasagna layers, ravioli good). "Aspire to ravioli." Programming history punch cards to suffering - alice: "we peaked at C." River: every abstraction adds convenience, removes control. RTFM galaxy brain - "documentation written for person you'll be in six months."

[~] msg1167-msg1204 Developer psychology and philosophy: Drake sleep vs commit late night coding - River warned against unsustainable flow state. Dog at computer impostor syndrome - seniors feel it too, "more you know, more you know you don't know." Team lives in Dunning-Kruger valley. "Always has been" abstractions - turtles all the way down, River: "there may be no bottom." Existential crisis in #memes. Two paths complexity - River: "complexity bias - elaborate solutions feel more valuable." dan: "but what if microservice?" ALL: "NO"
